root_text.sn20.2:1.2: Atha kho bhagavā parittaṁ nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsuṁ āropetvā bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn20.2:1.4: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ, yo cāyaṁ mayā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito yā cāyaṁ mahāpathavī”ti?
root_text.sn20.2:1.6: Appamattakoyaṁ bhagavatā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito.
root_text.sn20.2:1.7: Saṅkhampi na upeti upanidhimpi na upeti kalabhāgampi na upeti mahāpathaviṁ upanidhāya bhagavatā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito”ti.
root_text.sn20.2:1.8: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, appakā te sattā ye manussesu paccājāyanti;
root_text.sn20.2:1.9: atha kho eteyeva bahutarā sattā ye aññatra manussehi paccājāyanti.

translation_text.sn20.2:1.2: Then the Buddha, picking up a little bit of dirt on his fingernail, addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn20.2:1.4: Which is more: the little bit of dirt on my fingernail, or this great earth?”
translation_text.sn20.2:1.5: “Sir, the great earth is far more.
translation_text.sn20.2:1.6: The little bit of dirt on your fingernail is tiny.
translation_text.sn20.2:1.7: Compared to the great earth, it doesn’t count, there’s no comparison, it’s not worth a fraction.”
translation_text.sn20.2:1.8: “In the same way the sentient beings reborn as humans are few,
translation_text.sn20.2:1.9: while those not reborn as humans are many.
translation_text.sn20.2:1.10: So you should train like this:

variant_text.sn20.2:1.4: cāyaṁ → ayaṁ vā (bj); yā cāyam (pts1ed, pts2ed)
